page: Microsoft Azure L64s_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the LSv3 family, virtualized via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 16,015 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the L64s_v3 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by high scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is also robust, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths placing in the top tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also register strong benchmark results, reflecting the high-throughput capabilities of the storage and memory subsystems.

While multi-threaded and high-throughput operations are highly performant, single-core benchmarks, such as single-threaded gzip compression and single-core Geekbench scores, fall into the average range. Additionally, large-scale LLM inference tasks, particularly text generation with 70B models, show weak performance due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. Given its massive local storage capacity, high memory allocation, and strong multi-core processing, the L64s_v3 is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for large relational databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput transactional applications.
description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v3 is a storage-optimized server from the LSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 16,015 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside high-throughput database and Redis SET operations. However, single-core processing and large-scale LLM inference remain average to weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is highly cost-efficient for data-intensive, high-capacity storage applications. It is ideally suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput transactional workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v3 is an LSv3 family server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16,015 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v3 is an LSv3 server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16,015 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-dense server featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and over sixteen terabytes of local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory paired with 16,015 GB of local storage
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Average single-core performance and weak large-scale LLM inference capabilities
Optimized for large-scale databases, NoSQL platforms, and data warehousing
